message.content: Adversaries may gather victim IP addresses to facilitate targeting and reconnaissance. Public IP addresses, often allocated in blocks, can reveal details such as organizational size, physical locations, and Internet service providers. This information can be collected through methods like Active Scanning, Phishing for Information, or searching open technical databases. Once obtained, IP addresses can aid in further reconnaissance, establishing operational resources, or gaining initial access. For instance, Andariel has targeted specific IP ranges in watering hole attacks, while HAFNIUM has focused on IP addresses of publicly-accessible Exchange servers. Mitigation strategies include monitoring and restricting access to IP address information, employing network segmentation, and using intrusion detection systems to identify and respond to suspicious scanning activities.
